title: AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SAN LUIS, PROVINCE OF AGUSAN DEL SUR, TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY DOÃA MAXIMA

chunks.text:
118 OG No. 32, 8976 (August 8, 2022)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 11903, July 28, 2022 ]

AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SAN LUIS, PROVINCE OF AGUSAN DEL SUR, TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY DOÃA MAXIMA

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Creation of Barangay

Doña Maxima. -

There is hereby created a barangay in the Municipality of San Luis, Province of Agusan del Sur, to be known as Barangay

Doña Maxima.

SEC. 2.

Territorial Boundaries. -

The territorial boundaries of Barangay

Doña Maxima

are hereby described as follows:

SEC. 3.

Conduct and Supervision of Plebiscite. -

The plebiscite conducted and supervised by the Commission on Elections (COMELEC) pursuant to Provincial Ordinance No. 07, Series of 1991 of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan of the Province of Agusan del Sur, which ratified the creation of Barangay

Doña

Maxima

as proclaimed by the Plebiscite Board of Canvassers on December 19, 1998, shall serve as substantial compliance with the plebiscite requirement under Section 10 of the Local Government Code of 1991, as amended.

SEC. 4.

Incumbent Barangay Officials. -

The incumbent elected barangay officials of Barangay

Doña Maxima

shall continue to hold office until such time their successors have been duly elected and qualified.

SEC. 5.

Public Infrastructure and Facilities. -

All public infrastructure and facilities existing therein at the time of the approval of this Act are hereby transferred without cost or compensation to and shall be administered by Barangay

Doña Maxima.

SEC. 6.

National Tax Allotment (NTA) Shares. -

Barangay Doña Maxima shall be entitled to internal revenue allotment (IRA) shares pursuant to Section 285 of the Local Government Code of 1991, as amended.

SEC. 7.

Repealing Clause. -

All laws, decrees, executive orders, rules and regulations contrary to or inconsistent with any provision of this Act are hereby repealed or amended accordingly.

SEC. 8.

Effectivity. -

This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.)

VICENTE C. SOTTO III

President of the Senate

(SGD.)

LORD ALAN JAY Q. VELASCO

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate of the Philippines on September 27, 2021 and May 30, 2022.

(SGD.)

MYRA MARIE D. VILLARICA

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.)

MARK LLANDRO L. MENDOZA

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: Lapsed into law on JUL 28 2022 without the signature of the President, in accordance with Article VI Section 27 (1) of the Constitution.

(SGD.)

RODRIGO ROA DUTERTE

President of the Philippines
